metrics.narrative: The 21 Jan 2026 on-site (Avoma, 'External - Allstate and Zycus Product Session') covered a two-phase SAP lift-and-shift (6 months Source-to-Contract, 3 months Procure-to-Pay, ~9 months total) and framed Merlin's autonomous negotiation as driving 'tactical savings,' but no Allstate-specific quantified dollar impact has been buyer-confirmed. Pain is well documented (manual CLM redlining, disparate supplier-risk/contract-renewal systems, non-PO invoice matching gaps) from the June 2025 demos, but it has not yet been converted into a dated business case.

champion.narrative: Gaurav (Rakesh Desai) has functioned as the deal's champion since at least Dec 2025 - facilitating the Jan 21, 2026 onsite, connecting internally on POC/commercials (26 May 2026: 'gaurav connecting w leadership on next steps with POC, commercials, etc.'), and being the sole conduit to Casey McDowell. However, he has gone unresponsive on the single most important open question (SAP beta status) for over a month: no update on 8 Jun, explicitly 'no update on the beta or evaluation from gaurav' on 16 Jun, and still no confirmed reply as of the 29 Jun 2026 Next Step entry. This is a weakening, at-risk champion relationship, not a confirmed strong one.

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the confirmed, active, and dominant threat: 15 Dec 2025 explicitly frames the choice as 'SAP VS Zycus,' and as of 26 May 2026 Allstate 'is currently still doing a SAP Ariba workshop/beta program' before any next step on Zycus's POC or commercials can move. Coupa and iValua sit in the Salesforce Competitors field and surfaced only as generic 'what made you look at an alternate' discovery questions in the Jun 2025 demos, with no recent (last 90-day) evidence of active evaluation - they read as background/dormant, not current threats.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is thoroughly documented across the June 2025 demo series: manual Word-based contract redlining and check-in/out ('Contract approvals and redlining are managed manually using Microsoft Word,' 25 Jun 2025), manual supplier onboarding via Ariba with credit/adverse-media checks, non-PO invoices (notably tax payments) that don't tie back to POs or contracts despite a $20,000 compliance threshold (26 Jun 2025), and disparate homegrown tools for contract renewal and supplier risk tracking. This is confirmed, buyer-articulated pain, not inference.

paper_process.narrative: No commercial, legal, or InfoSec activity has begun. The Next Step trail through 29 Jun 2026 shows the deal still waiting on the SAP beta outcome before any paper process can start; the existing commercial proposal is roughly a year old and has been internally flagged as outdated in three separate rep emails without a refresh being sent to the buyer.

sources: OpportunityContactRole (Casey McDowell, Cheryl Harris - Executive Sponsor)
economic_buyer.narrative: Casey McDowell (VP, Global Enterprise Procurement) and Cheryl Harris (SVP, Procurement) are both mapped as Executive Sponsor contact roles, but neither has appeared on a recorded call or confirmed direct engagement. Next Step entries show the deal owner requesting an executive session with Casey since 4 Feb 2026 ('working on setting up executive session with Casey in Chicago') and again 18 Feb 2026 ('asking for executive meeting w Casey/cheryl'); as of the 29 Jun 2026 Next Step entry no session has been confirmed as completed, meaning access to power is identified but unengaged roughly 5 months later.

decision_process.narrative: On the 21 Jan 2026 onsite the buyer set its own target: 'decision in next 3/4 months' (i.e., by roughly April-May 2026). That window has now passed by about 2-3 months with no new decision date communicated. The process is explicitly gated on Allstate's internal SAP Ariba beta/workshop program (first flagged 15 Dec 2025: 'confirmed SAP VS Zycus'), whose conclusion the buyer has not reported despite follow-ups on 26 May, 8 Jun, and 16 Jun 2026 ('no update on the beta or evaluation from gaurav'). There is also a latent forcing function: the current Ariba agreement was noted 21 Jan 2026 as expiring roughly 12 months out (~Jan 2027), which is not an imminent driver.

decision_criteria.narrative: The June 2025 demo series (three sessions, 23/25/26 Jun) walked the evaluation team through sourcing, CLM, P2P, category management, and supplier onboarding against an active RFP scoring process ('what kind of scoring process do you follow internally, and how involved is the business'). The 21 Jan 2026 onsite added AI capability, roadmap, support, and implementation as explicit focus areas ('Focus areas - roadmap, product enhancements, support, implementation' per the 15 Jan 2026 Next Step note). Criteria are broadly known but not confirmed as finalized or weighted.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A large, long-running deal (open since March 2024) that reached Shortlisted with genuine depth of discovery and a strong January 2026 executive-facing onsite, but has since cooled: the buyer's own decision window has passed, the champion has gone quiet on the one question that matters, and the close date has no supporting late-stage evidence behind it.

deal_risk.text: The recorded close date of 31 Jul 2026 (22 days out) is not achievable: no POC has started, no updated commercials have been sent, and InfoSec/legal have not begun.

win_position.text: Deep, multi-session technical validation occurred across three demos in June 2025 plus a full-day onsite on 21 Jan 2026, giving Zycus real credibility on CLM, P2P, category management, and AI/lift-and-shift migration.

The buyer set its own decision target of 'next 3-4 months' on 21 Jan 2026 (i.e., by roughly Apr-May 2026); that window has passed with no new date, and the deal remains gated on Allstate's internal SAP Ariba beta.
Neither mapped Executive Sponsor (Casey McDowell, Cheryl Harris) has had direct engagement despite the deal owner requesting sessions since 4 Feb 2026.

deal_momentum.text: The champion (Gaurav) has not answered the deal owner's core question - SAP beta status - across three consecutive follow-ups on 26 May, 8 Jun, and 16 Jun 2026.
The last confirmed two-way buyer touch was an email reply on 22 May 2026 (48 days before this sweep); all activity since has been rep-initiated follow-up.

customer_commitment.text: Five reference calls (including two AI-at-scale customers) were committed as a next step on 21 Jan 2026 and remain unscheduled roughly 5.6 months later.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus has led with AI/Merlin maturity (autonomous negotiation, AI-driven CLM redlining, spend-analytics AI) and non-SAP-native, multi-ERP migration capability across the demo series and the Jan 2026 onsite, positioning directly against SAP Ariba's newer Joule AI. The differentiation argument remains unproven to the buyer because the promised AI-at-scale reference calls have not yet been delivered.

items.quote: The demo showcased robust AI-powered CLM capabilities including automated redlining assistance, a clause/template library, and seamless Microsoft Word integration.
Implementation will follow a two-phase lift-and-shift from SAP over approximately nine months (six months for Source-to-Contract and three months for Procure-to-Pay).

hard.ais_why:
• The 1/21/2026 Allstate on-site captures a direct buyer-driven AI action: Zycus to schedule and prep two reference calls with customers actively using Zycus AI capabilities, and share the reference list and call details with Allstate. The buyer is asking specifically for AI references, not generic ones.
• Same call records significant AI advancements across the S2P suite (a new agent-based intake manager and an autonomous negotiation flow for tactical savings) and notes that Zycus scrapped three non-AI references and lined up two new customers with high AI adoption appetite. The buyer pushed for that swap.
• An active ANA POC track is documented across 2/25, 3/2, and 4/13/2026, with a planned discussion of the next reference, the ANA POC, and updated commercials. The $1.5M deal is replacing SAP Ariba, with executive sessions in India and Chicago, Merlin in the product set, and an 11/24/2025 entry waiting on a response about key AI data and next steps.
